architecture_summary: MaskGIT is a bidirectional transformer decoder for image synthesis that learns Masked Visual Token Modeling (MVTM): during training it predicts randomly masked visual tokens, and at inference it generates all tokens in parallel and refines them iteratively with a decreasing mask ratio.

novel_contributions: a novel image synthesis paradigm using a bidirectional transformer decoder
Masked Visual Token Modeling (MVTM) for training on randomly masked visual tokens
a novel non-autoregressive iterative decoding method that generates all tokens simultaneously and refines them over a few iterations
a new masking strategy / mask scheduling function, with cosine performing best
extensions to image editing tasks such as inpainting, outpainting, and image manipulation

key_claims.claim: MaskGIT learns to predict randomly masked tokens during training and generates images by parallel iterative refinement at inference time.
MaskGIT significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art transformer model on the ImageNet dataset and accelerates autoregressive decoding by up to 64x.
MaskGIT is readily extendable to image editing tasks such as inpainting, extrapolation, and image manipulation.
MaskGIT finishes decoding in 8 iterations compared to the 256 rounds the sequential method takes.
On ImageNet 256×256, MaskGIT significantly outperforms VQGAN in FID and IS without special sampling strategies.
MaskGIT establishes a new state-of-the-art on CAS on the ImageNet benchmark on both resolutions.
MaskGIT achieves state-of-the-art FID on 512×512 ImageNet and state-of-the-art FID and IS on outpainting.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.2. Class-conditional Image Synthesis; 4.3. Image Editing Applications

key_claims.claim: Cosine masking schedule works the best in all of the experiments and is used as the default.
